questions.question: In an auction with an arbitrary distribution of valuations, which of the following players is most likely to win the auction?
options: A) Player with the highest valuation
B) Player with the lowest valuation
C) Player with an average valuation
D) Player with a valuation that is the median of all players
questions.explanation: The player with the highest valuation is more likely to win the auction, as they are more likely to be the highest bidder.

questions.question: In an auction with an arbitrary distribution of valuations, what is the probability that a player with a valuation of 50 will win the auction?
questions.explanation: If the distribution of valuations is arbitrary, the probability that a player with a valuation of 50 will win the auction is 0.25, as the distribution is symmetric and the player's valuation is just as likely as any other valuation.

questions.question: In an auction with an arbitrary distribution of valuations, what is the expected value of the winner's valuation?
questions.explanation: If the distribution of valuations is arbitrary, the expected value of the winner's valuation is 37.5, as the distribution is symmetric and the median valuation is 37.5.

questions.question: In an auction with an arbitrary distribution of valuations, what is the effect of a player's valuation on their probability of winning the auction?
options: A) Higher valuation increases probability of winning
B) Lower valuation decreases probability of winning
C) Higher valuation decreases probability of winning
D) Lower valuation increases probability of winning
questions.explanation: If the distribution of valuations is arbitrary, a player's valuation has no effect on their probability of winning the auction, as the distribution is symmetric and all players have the same probability of winning.

questions.question: In an auction with an arbitrary distribution of valuations, what is the optimal strategy for a player?
options: A) Bid the minimum price
B) Bid the maximum price
C) Bid a price that is the median of all players
D) Bid a price that is your true valuation
questions.explanation: If the distribution of valuations is arbitrary, the optimal strategy for a player is to bid a price that is the median of all players, as this maximizes the expected utility of winning the auction.
